data.title: U.S. Energy surges after signing five-year helium supply deal for Montana plant
data.description:
U.S. Energy (USEG) up 28.3% pre-market Monday after saying it signed a five-year helium sales agreement with an unnamed "global, investment-grade industrial gas company" for the sale of contained helium to be produced at its Big Sky Carbon Hub in Montana.

U.S. Energy (USEG) said the agreement establishes long-term contracted cash flow to support Phase 1 commercial operations, which remain targeted for Q1 2027.

The deal calls for fully contracted Phase 1 helium volumes of as much as 1.2M cf/month at fixed pricing of $285/Mcf of contained helium on an all-in plant-gate basis, the company said.

"This contract establishes long-term, contracted helium revenues and meaningfully de-risks Phase 1 commercial operations at Big Sky," U.S. Energy (USEG) President and CEO Ryan Smith said. "It also reflects the strength we’re seeing in the helium market today, where constrained global supply and increasing demand for reliable volumes are supporting a step up in long-term pricing."

data.title: Verizon's promos lead to 55K new wireless subscribers; raises FY26 profit guidance
data.description:
Verizon Communications (VZ) is propped up in early Monday trade as first-quarter results  reflected better-than-expected profits and postpaid phone additions, underscoring the company’s successful efforts to lure in new wireless customers despite intense competition from rivals AT&T (T) and T-Mobile (TMUS).

Shares last traded more than 4% higher in premarket.

The 55K increase in new wireless subscribers—a first for Q1—and 341K broadband net additions led Verizon (VZ) to anticipate total retail postpaid phone net additions for FY26 to be at the upper end of its forecast of 750K to 1M, and profits to be within a range of $4.95 to $4.99 per share, representing growth of 5% to 6% and exceeding the $4.91 consensus forecast.  This compares to prior guidance of $4.90 to $4.95 per share.

For FY26, total mobility and broadband service revenue is expected to increase by 2% to 3% to $93B, while wireless revenue is expected to be flat.

For the first quarter, Verizon (VZ) earned an adjusted profit of $1.28 per share, up 7.6% from the same quarter last year, $0.07 better than anticipated, and the best quarterly growth rate since 2021. 

Led by gains in mobility and broadband, total revenue increased 2.7% to $34.4B but missed expectations by $490M as the January service outage shaved 80 basis points off Verizon's (VZ) revenue growth rate for the quarter. 

On the balance sheet, cash flow from operating activities increased by 2.6% to $8.0B, while free cash flow increased by 4% to $3.6B. However, increased spending on promotions raised the company’s debt load, resulting in an unsecured debt-to-consolidated net income ratio of 8x versus 7.4x in the prior quarter.

data.title: Amazon inks book and podcast pact with Oprah Winfrey, NYT says
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) is said to have reached a multiyear licensing deal with Oprah Winfrey. The former doyenne of daytime talk will produce twice-a-week video podcasts beginning this summer, create specials focused on her Favorite Things and Book Club labels, and repurpose the 25-season library from The Oprah Winfrey Show, NYT reported on Monday.

The deal is similar in scope to the $100M agreement Amazon (AMZN) reached in 2024 for Jason and Travis Kelce’s podcast “New Heights” and signifies the company’s growing ambitions in video podcasts, the report added.

“This is the ultimate validation of where the world is going,” said Steve Boom, an Amazon vice president. “You have the most influential talk show host in history, by orders of magnitude, leaning heavily into this new world. Now we see where the world is going and we at Amazon are just happy to be along for the ride.”

Winfrey, 72, in the past few years has dabbled in podcasting with “The Oprah Podcast” channel on YouTube (GOOG) while pursuing other endeavors including producing “The Color Purple” film for Warner Bros. (WBD) and sharing her weight loss journey while on GLP-1 drugs.

Beginning in July, the video podcasts will also be available across Amazon’s services including Prime Video, Amazon Music, Fire TV Channels and Audible, the report said.

“Hosting this podcast allows me to continue the work I feel called to do — opening the door for conversations that matter,” Ms. Winfrey said in a statement to NYT. “Expanding our reach globally is an opportunity I embrace, as we continue to connect through stories that invite new ways of seeing, and hopefully deepen, understanding.”

In August, Amazon split its podcast company, Wondery, into two businesses. Sales and advertising revenue will be shared between Winfrey and the streaming giant.

In addition, Amazon plans to promote Winfrey’s popular book club, which has its 30th anniversary in September.

data.title: Joby Aviation shows off its electric air taxi capabilities in New York City
data.description:
Joby Aviation (JOBY) is attracting attention after completing the first-ever point-to-point electric air taxi demonstration flights in New York City across the city’s heliport network. The flights connected JFK with Manhattan sites, including Downtown Skyport and heliports in Midtown, and displayed how the quiet, zero-operating-emissions aircraft could eventually link airports, vertiports, and neighborhoods across the region.

The Santa Cruz, California-based company said the demonstrations, which will continue all week, are meant to prove the concept of fast urban air travel, with a potential trip from Lower Manhattan or Midtown to JFK taking under 10 minutes. Joby (JOBY) described the flights as part of a broader push toward commercial air taxi service, supported by partnerships with Delta Air Lines (DAL) and Uber Technology (UBER) and by its 2025 acquisition of Blade Air Mobility’s passenger business.

New York officials and transportation leaders called the flights an important step toward integrating next-generation aviation into the region’s infrastructure. "This cutting-edge aircraft is exactly the kind of innovation we have a responsibility to test, understand, and help shape for the good of the region and the public. These flights advance our work to determine how next-generation aviation technology can serve the people of New York and New Jersey," highlighted Port Authority Chairman Kevin O’Toole.

The flights in New York City continue Joby's (JOBY) 2026 Electric Skies Tour, which is a national showcase timed to celebrate the United States’ 250th anniversary and already included a landmark flight over the Golden Gate Bridge.

Shares of Joby Aviation (JOBY) edged 0.8% higher in premarket trading. The eVTOL stock is down more than 35% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Viper Energy leads all large-cap energy stocks in YoY CapEx growth
data.description:
A rising capital expenditure figure often signals that a company is betting heavily on its own future growth. Below is a list of U.S. large-cap energy stocks ranked by their year-over-year CapEx growth percentage, spanning multiple sectors and market capitalizations.

The list spans two primary sub-sectors within energy: Oil and Gas Exploration and Production, and Oil and Gas Storage and Transportation, covering companies across a broad range of market capitalizations.

Viper Energy (VNOM) tops the list with the highest CapEx growth rate of 248.28% year-over-year, reflecting the company's aggressive mineral acquisition strategy in the Permian Basin, where it nearly doubled its acreage in 2025.

Diamondback Energy (FANG) and Texas Pacific Land Corporation (TPL) follow in second and third place with growth rates of 104.36% and 100.47% respectively, while Expand Energy Corporation (EXE) and Ovintiv (OVV) round out the top five at 88.25% and 86.76%.

The Williams Companies (WMB) and MPLX LP (MPLX) occupy the sixth and seventh positions with CapEx growth of 86.67% and 71.21%, reflecting significant pipeline and midstream infrastructure investment.

ONEOK (OKE) and Energy Transfer (ET) follow at 55.96% and 51.37%, while Antero Resources (AR) rounds out the list at 49.73%. Notably, every stock on this list carries a Hold Quant Rating, with scores ranging from 3.12 to 3.46, suggesting the quant models see the sector's aggressive spending as largely priced in rather than as a near-term catalyst for outperformance.

Here is the list:

  1. Viper Energy, Inc. (VNOM), CapEx growth YoY: 248.28%

  2. Diamondback Energy, Inc. (FANG), CapEx growth YoY: 104.36%

  3. Texas Pacific Land Corporation (TPL), CapEx growth YoY: 100.47%

  4. Expand Energy Corporation (EXE), CapEx growth YoY: 88.25%

  5. Ovintiv Inc. (OVV), CapEx growth YoY: 86.76%

  6. The Williams Companies, Inc. (WMB), CapEx growth YoY: 86.67%

  7. MPLX LP Common Units (MPLX), CapEx growth YoY: 71.21%

  8. ONEOK, Inc. (OKE), CapEx growth YoY: 55.96%

  9. Energy Transfer LP Common Units (ET), CapEx growth YoY: 51.37%

  10. Antero Resources Corporation (AR), CapEx growth YoY: 49.73%

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC)

data.title: Sagimet Biosciences rockets 30% on pricing $175M equity offering
data.description:
• Clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company Sagimet Biosciences (SGMT) stock price jumped about 30% on Monday pre-market hours as the company priced an underwritten offering of ~29M shares at $6.00 per share.
  • The gross proceeds of $175M. Net proceeds combined with existing capital will be used to fund a Phase 3 trial for denifanstat in acne, advance TVB-3567 and a topical FASN inhibitor, and cover general corporate expenses. The company expects this funding to sustain its acne program operations and support the Phase 3 trial readout through 2028.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about April 28, 2026.

data.title: Applied Optoelectronics leads all large-cap IT stocks in YoY CapEx growth
data.description:
A rising capital expenditure figure often signals that a company is betting heavily on its own future growth. Below is a list of U.S. large-cap IT stocks ranked by their year-over-year CapEx growth percentage, spanning multiple sectors and market capitalizations.

The list spans multiple sectors within technology, including Communications Equipment, Semiconductors, Application Software, Systems Software, Electronic Manufacturing Services, and Semiconductor Materials & Equipment.

Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) tops the list with a CapEx growth rate of 318.80% year-over-year, reflecting the company's rapid infrastructure expansion amid surging demand for high-speed optical networking components.

Arista Networks (ANET) and MACOM Technology Solutions (MTSI) follow in second and third place with growth rates of 273.44% and 241.13% respectively, while Strategy Inc (MSTR) and Oracle (ORCL) round out the top five at 235.45% and 223.11%.

Guidewire Software (GWRE) and FormFactor (FORM) occupy the sixth and seventh positions with CapEx growth of 206.37% and 169.69%, while Palantir Technologies (PLTR) follows at 168.18%. Palo Alto Networks (PANW) and Sanmina Corporation (SANM) round out the list at 130.88% and 130.73% respectively.

Notably, Sanmina (SANM) is the only stock on the list to carry a Buy Quant Rating of 4.07, a meaningful distinction in a group where most names hold Hold ratings, and Strategy Inc (MSTR) sits closest to bearish territory with a Hold rating of 2.59.

Here is the list:

  1. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. (AAOI), CapEx growth YoY: 318.80%

  2. Arista Networks, Inc. (ANET), CapEx growth YoY: 273.44%

  3. MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings, Inc. (MTSI), CapEx growth YoY: 241.13%

  4. Strategy Inc (MSTR), CapEx growth YoY: 235.45%

  5. Oracle Corporation (ORCL), CapEx growth YoY: 223.11%

  6. Guidewire Software, Inc. (GWRE), CapEx growth YoY: 206.37%

  7. FormFactor, Inc. (FORM), CapEx growth YoY: 169.69%

  8. Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR), CapEx growth YoY: 168.18%

  9. Palo Alto Networks, Inc. (PANW), CapEx growth YoY: 130.88%

  10. Sanmina Corporation (SANM), CapEx growth YoY: 130.73%

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT)

data.title: Ligand to buy XOMA Royalty for $39/share
data.description:
Ligand Pharmaceuticals (LGND) has agreed to acquire XOMA Royalty (XOMA) for $39.00 per share of common stock in cash, for a total equity value of approximately $739M.

The transaction has been unanimously approved by the Ligand and XOMA Royalty boards of directors and is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions.

The cash consideration for the transaction is expected to be funded with Ligand’s existing cash on hand and borrowings under its existing credit facility.

The transaction is expected to be immediately accretive to Ligand’s earnings per share. The company raised its 2026 revenue guidance to $270M-$310M from $245M-$285M and increased adjusted diluted EPS guidance to $8.50-$9.50 from $8.00-$9.00. Ligand also expects the deal to add $1.50 per share to adjusted EPS in 2027.

The transaction also adds more than 120 commercial, clinical, and preclinical assets to Ligand’s royalty portfolio. As a result, the company raised its royalty revenue outlook to $225M-$250M from $200M-$225M.

"The acquisition of XOMA Royalty presents a compelling opportunity for us to strengthen and diversify our portfolio across all stages of clinical development and accelerate our long-term profitable growth. This acquisition will add seven marketed products and nearly double our portfolio of Phase 2 and 3 assets, which we believe will create significant value for our stockholders, all through a single transaction,” said Todd Davis, CEO of Ligand.

Ligand will hold an investor call at 8:00 a.m. ET today to discuss today’s announcement. LGND and XOMA both rose 2.9% premarket.

data.title: Alibaba rolls out viral Happy Horse AI model in beta
data.description:
• Alibaba (BABA) said on Monday that it has rolled out the Happy Horse AI model, which went viral earlier this year, in beta.
  • “We’re excited to roll out HappyHorse 1.0 in limited beta,” Alibaba wrote on X. “It is now accessible to creators globally via the HappyHorse official website or through API service on Alibaba Cloud Model Studio.”
  • Shares of the Chinese tech giant fell in premarket trading.

data.title: Astec declares $0.13 dividend
data.description:
